source_claim: Failed rule, pattini praise, fate and the anklet govern the epic
Yavana residences and ship-borne migrants form a cosmopolitan port quarter
Colour, lime, sandal, flowers and incense are traded
Silk, hair or wool and cotton support textile production
Cloth, coral, sandal, akil, pearls, gems and gold are concentrated
Classified goods and grain are stored in a dedicated street
Fish, salt, meat and prepared-food sellers form a dense market
Metal, wood, clay, jewellery, tailoring and leather crafts are organised
Musicians master flute, yāḻ, voice and the seven notes
Commodity, craft, food and music streets define Maruvūrppākkam
Royal and bannered-chariot streets form a ceremonial axis
Merchants, ritual specialists, physicians and artisans occupy distinct residences
Bards, heralds, timekeepers, dancers and attendants support court life
Horsemen, mahouts, charioteers and warriors occupy a service quarter
Royal, merchant, professional and military functions define Paṭṭiṉappākkam
The day market links the two major quarters
Food, flowers, incense and dance support civic protection ritual
Warriors from both quarters perform public victory ritual
Five civic forums serve custody, healing, justice and exposure
Flags, drums, elephants, lamps and councils mobilise the city
Multiple sacred institutions, charity and public music coexist
Festival streets remain awake with crowds, music and pleasure
Eleven codified dances and associated songs form the repertoire
Bathing, fragrance, clothing and detailed ornaments form a staged costume sequence
Mādhavi and Kōvalaṉ move through decorated streets to the shore
Profession-specific lamps identify sellers, boats, lighthouse and guards
Goods, ships, elites, merchants and performers crowd the shore
A temporary painted pavilion creates elite privacy within the public beach
Moon, sun, rain, Kāviri country and Pukār frame royal abundance
Kaṇṇaki belongs to a wealthy and respected merchant family
Kōvalaṉ is heir to another wealthy merchant household
The wedding uses elephants, drums, conches, pavilion, lamps and public display
Ships and land transport feed an abundant merchant city
Upper-storey rooms, windows, terraces, beds and floral interiors shape elite domestic life
The couple are established separately with hospitality, kin and household duties
Mādhavi trains seven years in dance, song and beauty before royal debut
Dance, music, yāḻ, flute, percussion and poetry require specialist teachers
The stage has measured dimensions, entrances, lamps, curtains and painted canopy
Royal procession, sacred water, drums and councils inaugurate the stage
Flute, yāḻ, percussion and ensemble follow a formal performance sequence
Royal reward and garland sale connect prestige, payment and patronage
Lamps, flowers, music and lovers mark the city’s evening transition
Mādhavi prepares for union while Kaṇṇaki removes ornament in separation
Birds, drums, conches and guards carry the city from night to dawn
Kōvalaṉ performs through named yāḻ techniques before singing
River abundance, kingship and chastity are joined in song
Fishing settlement, drying fish, nets, sand and sea labour shape the imagery
Mādhavi interprets hidden meaning and answers through the same musical form
Kōvalaṉ treats Mādhavi’s performance as deceit and leaves
Summer links Madurai, Uraiyur, Vañji and Pukār within one Tamil political world
Mādhavi tunes, tests modes and performs as a technically expert musician
Mādhavi writes a message and sends it through Vasantamālai
Named performance forms encode Mādhavi’s appeal and Kōvalaṉ rejects the message
Kaṇṇaki’s social world includes numerous shrines and sacred establishments
Her dream anticipates accusation, royal confrontation and danger to the city
Kaṇṇaki rejects ritual bathing as an unsuitable way to recover her husband
Kōvalaṉ returns, sees Kaṇṇaki’s suffering and admits the loss of family wealth
Kaṇṇaki offers the anklet and the couple decide to rebuild wealth in Madurai
They leave through the long gate and pass Viṣṇu, Buddhist and Jain-associated sites
They cross the city edge, enter Kāviri groves and meet Kauṇṭi
